speaker: CADE
text: Up Fish Street! Down Saint Magnus’ Corner!

speaker: CADE
text: Kill and knock down! Throw them into Thames!

speaker: CADE
text: What noise is this I hear? Dare any be so bold to

speaker: CADE
text: sound retreat or parley when I command them

speaker: CADE
text: kill?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Ay, here they be that dare and will disturb thee.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Know, Cade, we come ambassadors from the King

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Unto the Commons, whom thou hast misled,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And here pronounce free pardon to them all

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That will forsake thee and go home in peace.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: What say you, countrymen? Will you relent

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And yield to mercy whil’st ’tis offered you,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Or let a rabble lead you to your deaths?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Who loves the King and will embrace his pardon,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Fling up his cap and say “God save his Majesty!”

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Who hateth him and honors not his father,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Henry the Fifth, that made all France to quake,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Shake he his weapon at us and pass by.

speaker: ALL
text: God save the King! God save the King!

speaker: CADE
text: What, Buckingham and Clifford, are you so

speaker: CADE
text: brave?—And, you base peasants, do you believe

speaker: CADE
text: him? Will you needs be hanged with your pardons

speaker: CADE
text: about your necks? Hath my sword therefore broke

speaker: CADE
text: through London gates, that you should leave me at

speaker: CADE
text: the White Hart in Southwark? I thought you

speaker: CADE
text: would never have given out these arms till you had

speaker: CADE
text: recovered your ancient freedom. But you are all

speaker: CADE
text: recreants and dastards, and delight to live in slavery

speaker: CADE
text: to the nobility. Let them break your backs with

speaker: CADE
text: burdens, take your houses over your heads, ravish

speaker: CADE
text: your wives and daughters before your faces. For

speaker: CADE
text: me, I will make shift for one, and so God’s curse

speaker: CADE
text: light upon you all!

speaker: ALL
text: We’ll follow Cade! We’ll follow Cade!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Is Cade the son of Henry the Fifth,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: That thus you do exclaim you’ll go with him?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Will he conduct you through the heart of France

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And make the meanest of you earls and dukes?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Alas, he hath no home, no place to fly to,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Nor knows he how to live but by the spoil,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Unless by robbing of your friends and us.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Were ’t not a shame that, whilst you live at jar,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: The fearful French, whom you late vanquishèd,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Should make a start o’er seas and vanquish you?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Methinks already in this civil broil

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: I see them lording it in London streets,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Crying “Villiago!” unto all they meet.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Better ten thousand baseborn Cades miscarry

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Than you should stoop unto a Frenchman’s mercy.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: To France, to France, and get what you have lost!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Spare England, for it is your native coast.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Henry hath money; you are strong and manly.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: God on our side, doubt not of victory.

speaker: ALL
text: À Clifford! À Clifford! We’ll follow the King and Clifford!

speaker: CADE
text: Was ever feather so lightly blown to and

speaker: CADE
text: fro as this multitude? The name of Henry the Fifth

speaker: CADE
text: hales them to an hundred mischiefs and makes

speaker: CADE
text: them leave me desolate. I see them lay their heads

speaker: CADE
text: together to surprise me. My sword make way for

speaker: CADE
text: me, for here is no staying!—In despite of the devils

speaker: CADE
text: and hell, have through the very middest of you!

speaker: CADE
text: And heavens and honor be witness that no want of

speaker: CADE
text: resolution in me, but only my followers’ base and

speaker: CADE
text: ignominious treasons, makes me betake me to my

speaker: CADE
text: heels.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: What, is he fled? Go, some, and follow him;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And he that brings his head unto the King

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Shall have a thousand crowns for his reward.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Follow me, soldiers. We’ll devise a means

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To reconcile you all unto the King.
